architectural_purpose: deterministic orchestration boundary and capability visibility without activation

blocked_reason_visibility: orchestration activation is blocked by descriptive-only capability governance

stale_reason_visibility: Phase 2 topology evidence is read-only ancestry for capability visibility

conflicting_reason_visibility: execution authorization conflicts with non-execution capability governance

diagnostics.finding: Capability identity is deterministic and non-executable.
Prohibited capabilities remain visible and inactive.
Unsupported capabilities remain visible and unavailable.
Blocked orchestration activation capabilities remain non-operational.
Stale topology context capabilities remain read-only ancestry.
Conflicting authorization capabilities remain blocked by non-execution governance.
Capability relationships to boundaries, policies, topology, and manifest remain descriptive.
Capability explainability documents unavailable activation, execution, planner integration, and operations.
Enabled operational capability count remains zero.

explainability_summaries.summary: Runtime execution is prohibited because capabilities are governance evidence only.
Future runtime coordination is unsupported until explicitly authorized by a later phase.
Orchestration activation is blocked because Phase 3 does not activate capabilities.
Prior topology context is stale read-only ancestry, not an operational source.
Execution authorization conflicts with the explicit non-execution capability boundary.
Capability activation cannot occur because enabled operational capability count is zero.
Execution remains unavailable across orchestration, runtime, capability, routing, traversal, dependency, sequencing, and scheduling surfaces.
Planner integration remains unavailable because capability visibility does not alter planner behavior.
Operational orchestration remains prohibited without explicit authorization in a later phase.
Governance boundaries exist to keep capability evidence deterministic, replay-safe, rollback-safe, and non-executable.

explicit_limitations: v4.3 Phase 3 creates deterministic orchestration capability governance modeling only.

v4.3 Phase 3 does not enable orchestration execution.
v4.3 Phase 3 does not enable orchestration activation.

v4.3 Phase 3 does not enable capability execution.
v4.3 Phase 3 does not enable routing, traversal, dependency, sequencing, or scheduling execution.
v4.3 Phase 3 does not enable authorization or readiness approval.
v4.3 Phase 3 does not enable remediation, repair, inference, recommendation, ranking, scoring, selection, or optimization.
v4.3 Phase 3 does not enable planning, decision, resolution, dispatch, or runtime coordination engines.
v4.3 Phase 3 does not enable planner integration.
v4.3 Phase 3 does not enable production consumption.
v4.3 Phase 3 does not enable runtime or operational state mutation.
explicit_prohibitions: No orchestration capability may become executable.
